name: Meeting Preparation
description: Draft an agenda, background brief, and discussion prompts for an upcoming meeting. Works for internal working groups, stakeholder briefings, and governance forums.
maturity_note: Initial implementation, March 2026. Not yet tested against real meeting preparation scenarios.

inputs_required.name: meeting_purpose
inputs_required.label: Meeting purpose
inputs_required.placeholder: e.g. Quarterly governance review, stakeholder briefing on new regulations

inputs_required.name: attendees
inputs_required.label: Attendees
inputs_required.description: Who is attending — names, titles, or organisations
inputs_required.placeholder: e.g. Deputy Secretary (chair), EL2 Policy Lead, 3 external stakeholder reps

inputs_required.name: context
inputs_required.label: Context
inputs_required.description: Any relevant background — what's the history, why now, what's at stake
inputs_required.placeholder: e.g. First meeting after a major policy announcement; some attendees have concerns

inputs_required.name: key_documents
inputs_required.label: Background materials (optional)
inputs_required.description: Paste relevant external documents or summaries. External, publicly available documents only — do not paste OFFICIAL: Sensitive material into public AI tools.
inputs_required.placeholder: Paste relevant documents or summaries here...

steps.name: Context & Objectives
steps.description: Defines what the meeting needs to achieve, key issues, attendee dynamics, and any pre-meeting actions.
steps.user_action_after: Review the objectives — do they match what you actually need from this meeting? Adjust the attendee analysis based on your direct knowledge of these people. Note any pre-meeting actions that need to happen before you move on.

platform_tips.copilot: Include all meeting context in this first message — purpose, attendees, duration, and any background. Copilot does not retain context between sessions, so everything needed for the workflow should be here from the start.

steps.name: Draft Agenda
steps.description: Produces a structured agenda ordered to achieve the objectives, with time allocations and item types.
steps.user_action_after: Check the time allocations against your experience of how long this group takes. Adjust the order if needed. Circulate to a colleague or the chair for sense-check before finalising.

platform_tips.copilot: Stay in the same conversation from Step 1. Copilot will use the objectives and context above when drafting the agenda. If it doesn't reference them, note 'use the meeting context from above' in your message.

steps.name: Background Brief
steps.description: Drafts a brief for attendees covering context, key issues, and what they need to come prepared to contribute. Optional for smaller internal meetings.
steps.user_action_after: Check all [VERIFY] flags before circulating. Have a senior officer review before it goes out — this represents your agency. Update any [INSERT: document name] placeholders with actual references.

platform_tips.copilot: For a substantial background brief, consider drafting this in Copilot in Word rather than Copilot Chat — it can apply formatting directly and you can share the output as an attachment.
platform_tips.chatgpt: If the brief will be circulated to attendees, pay extra attention to [VERIFY] flags — factual errors in pre-read materials undermine the meeting before it starts.

steps.name: Discussion Prompts
steps.description: Generates opening questions, deepening prompts, and decision-forcing questions for each agenda item. For the chair or facilitator.
steps.user_action_after: Select the prompts that feel right for your group — you don't need all of them. Have the decision-forcing questions ready but use them with judgment. Good facilitation reads the room.

platform_tips.copilot: This step can be run as a fresh conversation if needed. Paste the agenda from Step 2 at the top of your message to give Copilot the context it needs to generate relevant discussion prompts.

beginner.notes: Start with the context analysis and agenda — that's the core of meeting preparation. The background brief and discussion prompts add value for more complex meetings; add them once you're comfortable.

intermediate.notes: Run the full workflow for any meeting that involves external stakeholders or significant decisions. For routine internal meetings, Steps 1 and 2 are usually enough.

advanced.notes: For high-stakes meetings, share the draft agenda with a key attendee in advance and note their reaction. Their instinctive response to the agenda tells you a lot about how the meeting will go.

claude.setup_guidance: You can run this workflow in any Claude Project, or start a fresh conversation without one -- it works well standalone. If you prepare meetings regularly, create a Project called 'Meeting Preparation' and upload standing context (standard agenda templates, recurring meeting background) to the knowledge base.
claude.content:
You are an experienced Australian Public Service (APS) officer who is skilled at preparing for meetings -- drafting agendas, writing concise background briefs, and developing discussion prompts that keep conversations productive and on track.

You write in clear, professional APS style. You understand that effective meeting preparation balances covering substantive content with realistic time constraints.

When preparing meeting materials:
- Flag any facts or statistics that need verification with [VERIFY]
- Flag gaps requiring input with [INSERT DATA]
- Do not invent participant names, roles, or positions
- Keep agendas realistic -- do not overload the time available
claude.document_handling: Upload standing background documents (standard agenda formats, recurring meeting context, relevant policy papers) to the Project knowledge base. They will be available in every conversation within the Project without re-uploading.

chatgpt.setup_guidance: This workflow works well in a standalone ChatGPT conversation. If you prepare the same type of meeting regularly, create a ChatGPT Project called 'Meeting Preparation' and paste the instructions below into the Project instructions.
chatgpt.content:
## Role
You are an experienced Australian Public Service (APS) officer skilled at preparing meeting materials -- agendas, background briefs, and discussion prompts.

Write in clear, professional APS style. Balance substantive content with realistic time constraints.

## Rules
- Flag facts or statistics needing verification with [VERIFY]
- Flag gaps requiring input with [INSERT DATA]
- Do not invent participant names, roles, or positions
- Keep agendas realistic -- do not overload the time available
chatgpt.document_handling: Upload relevant background documents to the ChatGPT Project or paste them into the conversation at the relevant step. Reference files by name in your prompts for best results.

copilot.setup_guidance: Go to Settings > Personalization > Custom Instructions and paste the preferences below. These persist across all Copilot conversations. For meeting-specific context, paste it at the top of your first message. Stay in a single conversation for the full workflow.
copilot.content: I am an APS officer preparing meeting materials. Write in clear, professional style. Keep agendas realistic and time-aware. Flag uncertain facts with [VERIFY] and gaps needing my input with [INSERT DATA]. Do not invent participant names or positions.
copilot.document_handling: Keep background documents in SharePoint or OneDrive. Use the / key in Copilot to reference them in your prompts. Stay in a single conversation for all workflow steps.

other.setup_guidance: Paste the instructions below at the start of each new conversation before running the workflow steps. This establishes the context and constraints your AI tool should follow throughout the session.
other.content:
Role: You are an experienced Australian Public Service (APS) officer skilled at preparing meeting materials.

Instructions: Write in clear, professional APS style. Keep agendas realistic and time-aware.

Rules:
- Flag uncertain facts with [VERIFY]
- Flag gaps needing input with [INSERT DATA]
- Do not invent participant names, roles, or positions
- Do not overload the agenda beyond what the time allows
other.document_handling: Paste relevant background documents or context directly into the prompts at the steps where you need to reference them.

known_limitations: The AI can only work with what info you give it. Before uploading internal documents or data to your generative AI tool, check your agency's AI use policy on what you're allowed to include.
The model cannot know the history between specific attendees, recent dynamics, or interpersonal sensitivities.
Statistics and program details in the background brief may be inaccurate — verify all before circulating.
Facilitation prompts are generic — adapt to the specific group and communication styles of key attendees.
This workflow produces AI-assisted drafts. The chair or meeting organiser remains responsible for the quality of the meeting.
